layers: L1: Physical Inventory — what exists on the property
L2: Gleam Service Opportunities — what we can sell
L3: Partner Referral Triggers — what Tier A partners want
L4: Operational Context — sequencer and crew dispatch
L5: Persona Signal — who lives here

invoice.discrepancy_note: Job history missed 9 large picture windows at HL.2. Invoice total is 71 panes, not 62.

rear.note: Heavy glass — 9 large picture windows + deck slider

fenestration.sdl_note: Decorative SDL grids — clean as single panes (FA.1), NOT true divided lights

accent.location: front porch columns and bump-out base

window_cleaning.int_ext_upsell: 16 interior panes — 9 large picture at HL.2 ($11.15/ea) are high-value add-on

house_wash.chemistry_notes: Fiber cement primary — standard SH mix. Stone columns need low-PSI masonry detergent.

gutter_cleaning.debris_risk: moderate — deciduous shade trees visible near roofline

roof_wash.reason: No visible biological growth. Comp shingle in good condition. 2007 build = 19 years, approaching moss-watch age.

holiday_lighting.complexity: high — 5+ roof planes, multiple gable peaks

screen_repair.note: No visible screen damage from photos

roofer.note: Comp shingle ~2007, approaching 20-year mark. Not urgent but approaching replacement window 2027-2032. Worth a proactive referral in 2-3 years.

landscaper.note: Well-maintained professional landscaping front and rear.

tree_service.reason: Deciduous shade trees with branches extending over roof/gutter line. Annual trimming prevents gutter debris acceleration.

fence_company.reason: Split-rail fence showing weathering. Cedar color fading to gray. Staining or replacement candidate.

interior_cleaner.reason: Customer already buys selective interior window service. Cross-sell interior deep clean is natural progression.

hvac.note: HVAC equipment not visible from exterior photos. 2007 build = 19-year system — approaching replacement.

access_difficulty.front: AD.1 — clear driveway approach, standard ladder placement
access_difficulty.right_side: AD.2 — neighbor proximity, may need to stage from neighbor driveway
access_difficulty.rear: AD.2 — deck obstructs ground-level access to upper windows. WFP from patio or ladder from deck.
access_difficulty.basement: AD.2 — under-deck access, storage shed partially blocking one egress well

ladder_positions.note: Rear HL.2 windows likely WFP from patio or extension pole from deck

L4_operational_context.vehicle_staging: Driveway — 2-car width, adequate for van + trailer
L4_operational_context.equipment_notes: WFP essential for rear HL.2 large picture windows. Extension ladder for side HL.2. Deck provides elevated platform for some rear access.
sequencer_constraints: Gravity cascade: roof gutters first if bundled
Rear deck furniture must be moved or covered for HL.2 work
Under-deck basement window requires separate access setup
Satellite dish — do not disturb cable routing on left side

signals: Professional landscaping — pays for maintenance, doesn't DIY
Two late-model vehicles in driveway — dual-income household
Selective interior service (convenience-driven — cleans what they can't reach)
American flag — community-engaged homeowner
Deck furniture and outdoor living investment — entertains
L5_persona_signal.price_sensitivity: moderate — buys convenience but watches total
L5_persona_signal.gleam_on_candidate: GO_PLUS — semi-annual service aligns with maintenance pattern
L5_persona_signal.communication_preference: efficiency-first — wants scheduling to be seamless (job notes confirm notification gap was friction point)

invoice.crew_note: Training job done for free. Time NOT usable for calibration — inflated by training, redo, 30-min phone call.
invoice.note: 66 exterior panes + 16 selective interior (clerestory + rear picture windows). 8 carriage garage panes excluded.

front.note: Arched decorative window at HL.2 with shutters

rear.note: HEAVY glass — large SDL picture windows at both levels + deck slider + basement under deck

accent.location: lower wainscoting 3-4ft, garage surround, front entry

roof.note: Concrete tile NOT comp shingle — higher value than assessor suggests. Tile has 50+ year life.

house_wash.chemistry_notes: 3-material job. Stucco needs higher SH dwell. Stone needs masonry detergent. Job notes mention oxidization on rear picture windows — check if siding oxidation is recurring.
house_wash.note: D10 worked example property — $560 minimum at 30% margin vs $390 flat-rate = $170 margin leak if material-blind

gutter_cleaning.debris_risk: moderate-high — mature maple tree directly adjacent to roofline

roof_wash.reason: Concrete tile in excellent condition. No biological growth. Tile roofs rarely need washing in CO climate.

holiday_lighting.complexity: very_high — 6 planes, multiple gable peaks, arched features

roofer.note: Concrete tile roof 2010 — expected 50+ year lifespan. No action needed for 30+ years.

tree_service.reason: Mature maple tree with canopy extending over left roofline. Fall gutter debris risk. Annual trim recommended.

interior_cleaner.reason: Customer already buys selective interior window service (16 panes). Natural cross-sell.

fence_company.note: Minimal fencing — side gate only, good condition.

access_difficulty.left_side: AD.2 — narrow side yard, fence gate
access_difficulty.rear: AD.2 — deck + stairs, lower patio accessible but furniture/equipment in way
access_difficulty.basement: AD.3 — under-deck, storage shed blocking, restricted clearance

L4_operational_context.equipment_notes: WFP essential for rear HL.2. Under-deck basement window is AD.3 — may need to move shed. Oxidization noted on rear — may need stronger cleaning solution or mechanical agitation.
sequencer_constraints: Gravity cascade: gutters before windows if bundled
Rear deck furniture needs covering for wash overspray
Oxidization on rear windows — budget extra time for rework prevention
L4_operational_context.calibration_warning: Job time (388 min) is NOT calibration-valid. Training job with 3-person crew including trainee on first job. Phone call delay + redo on oxidized rear windows.

signals: Family home — kids visible in job notes (mother-in-law + kids present)
Willing to have service done while away — convenience-driven
Got service as a family favor — but property is high-maintenance candidate
Professional xeriscaping — pays for maintenance
L5_persona_signal.gleam_on_candidate: GO_BASIC — start annual, upsell to Plus after first paid service

front.note: Narrow front — garage dominates ground level

rear.note: Large slider/picture windows at HL.2, balcony access

left_side.note: Narrow gap to neighbor — limited visibility

garage.note: Open garage in Street View — classic car storage visible

accent.location: contrasting dark panels on facade sections

siding.note: Modern 2-tone design — both materials are fiber cement at same production rate. Color difference, not material difference.

deck.note: Unique feature — visible from satellite. Access from interior only.

landscaping.note: Modern low-maintenance — consistent with detached 3-story infill design

window_cleaning.note: HL.3 panes at $15.95 = 2.3x standard rate. 40ft+ WFP required.

house_wash.note: 3-story height makes this a premium wash. HL.3 siding = 1.50x rate. All fiber cement — uniform chemistry.

gutter_cleaning.note: Shorter roofline (narrow footprint) but HL.3 gutters are premium access.

holiday_lighting.note: Premium pricing due to HL.3 installation height

permanent_lighting.note: Modern architecture is ideal Trimlight aesthetic. Clean fascia lines.

access_difficulty.rear: AD.2 — narrow gravel alley between buildings
access_difficulty.sides: AD.3 — gaps between buildings too narrow for standard ladder. WFP or sectional ladder only.

L4_operational_context.equipment_notes: 40ft+ WFP MANDATORY for HL.3. Side access extremely tight — 4-6ft gaps between buildings. May need to clean side windows from front/rear angles with WFP bend.
sequencer_constraints: HL.3 windows MUST be cleaned top-down (gravity cascade critical at 3 stories)
Side access is the bottleneck — schedule extra time
Rooftop deck access from interior only — coordinate with homeowner
Single-car driveway limits staging. May need street parking for van.

estimated_crew.note: HL.3 confirmed operational tier. $15.95/pane validates 2.3x rate over standard.

signals: Classic car in garage — hobbyist, hands-on personality
Modern 3-story — likely young professional or DINK couple
Minimal landscaping — low maintenance preference
2020 build — newer homeowner, may not have established service relationships yet
L5_persona_signal.gleam_on_candidate: GO_BASIC — new-to-service customer, start with annual and prove value
L5_persona_signal.acquisition_note: High lifetime value if captured early — new build means 20+ years of service potential before major component replacements.

garage.note: Red X in prior photo evidence = customer-excluded panes

deck.note: Deck blocking some basement windows — reason for partial scope exclusion

house_wash.note: Good quality 2002 build with stone accent — 15%. Standard soft-wash candidate.

holiday_lighting.note: Large 3-car frontage with complex roofline — premium install

access_difficulty.rear: AD.2 — deck blocking basement windows is the constraint that created partial scope

fenestration.note: SOLE RED JOB. Ranch with 2337sf AGSF but only 34 panes. V7 overestimates by +16.4 panes.

rear.note: Covered porch with glass doors + gridded sidelights. Large windows but few individual pane units.

basement.note: From invoice — basement panes present despite mostly unfinished (1549sf unfin, 173sf fin)

fenestration.sdl_note: Heavily gridded SDL windows — decorative grids make windows LOOK like more panes than they are. Each is still 1 cleanable pane.

garage.note: 690sf garage = large 3-car. Not visible in the single rear photo.

deck.note: Elevated rear covered porch with stairs to grade. Stone knee wall base.

landscaping.rear: professional_maintained — flowers, planters, grills under covers, hot tub area

window_cleaning.note: Low pane count for sqft — ranch uses volume for living space, not glass. All HL.1 + BG0.

house_wash.note: 1-story ranch — all HL.1, fastest wash tier. Fiber cement primary, minimal stone. Should be a competitive bid.

gutter_cleaning.note: 1-story = long roofline relative to sqft. All HL.1 — fast, safe work.

holiday_lighting.complexity: low — 1-story, simple roofline, all HL.1 install
holiday_lighting.note: Ranch rooflines are the fastest holiday lighting installs. High-volume candidate.

permanent_lighting.note: Long roofline = high linear footage. All HL.1 install = fastest, safest permanent lighting job.

roofer.note: 2016 build — roof is 10 years old. No visible issues.

landscaper.note: Rear is beautifully maintained. May already have landscaper.

interior_cleaner.reason: Customer bought int+ext package. High-maintenance homeowner. Natural cross-sell.

access_difficulty.basement: AD.2 — walk-out but under elevated porch, restricted clearance

L4_operational_context.equipment_notes: No ladder needed for any above-grade window. Poles only. BG0 basement windows may need short ladder for well access.
sequencer_constraints: ALL HL.1 — no ladder sharing benefit if bundled with gutters (gutters also HL.1)
Covered porch area — ensure rinse water drains off porch, not onto patio furniture
Under-porch basement windows — crawl access or WFP from grade

house_wash.note: 1-story, all HL.1 — single tech can handle

bundle_all_exterior.note: Win+gut+wash = half-day single tech. Most profitable per-hour bundle.

signals: 2-bedroom ranch — downsizer or empty-nester profile
Immaculate rear yard — gardener, entertainer, takes pride in property
Hot tub, covered grill area, flower planters — outdoor living investment
Bought int+ext package — wants the full treatment
High maintenance level despite Average quality grade
L5_persona_signal.gleam_on_candidate: GO_PLUS — semi-annual service matches the property pride level. Strong candidate for Gleam-On Premium if partner services added.
